'''Wilst Molan''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Coruscanti/Legends|from]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] who frequented the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]] in the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]] of the planet. At the club, she and her acquaintances [[Nyrat Agira/Legends|Nyrat Agira]] and [[Di Mantid]] sought out a [[Currency/Legends|rich]] and powerful mate. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], she was inside the club and vied for the title of best-dressed at the club with Agira.

==Biography==
Wilst Molan was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Coruscanti/Legends|to]]<ref name="DB" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="aotcvis" /> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. She<ref name="DB" /> was a participant in Coruscant's party scene<ref name="Rogues">{{InsiderCite|136|Rogues Gallery|Rogues Gallery: A Night at the Outlander Club}}</ref> and a frequent patron of the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]],<ref name="DB" />  a [[Nightclub/Legends|nightclub]] and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] hall located in the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]] of Coruscant.<ref name="CSWE2">{{CSWECite|II|431|Outlander Club}}</ref> Molan and her acquaintances [[Nyrat Agira/Legends|Nyrat Agira]] and [[Di Mantid]] formed a regular group that visited the club in hopes of finding a [[Currency/Legends|rich]] and powerful mate.<ref name="FF">{{FactFile|96|OUT 1-2|Outlander Nightclub Patrons}}</ref> Molan often danced among the crowds of the club, which led to the assumption that she was a hired dancer for the club.<ref name="DB" />

[[File:Outlander_Club.png|thumb|300px|right|Molan frequented the Outlander Club (pictured).]]
One [[Local day/Legends|night]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> Molan was inside the club alongside Agira and Mantid<ref name="FF" /> and stood near the bar as [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] entered the club in [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala/Legends|search]] of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]]. Later, she stood near Skywalker as he looked for Wesell.<ref name="aotc">{{Film|II}}</ref> She vied for the title of best dressed between Agira and Mantid at the club. However, her [[Clothing/Legends|dress]] was not as charismatic as Agira's outfit.<ref name="FF" />

==Personality and traits==
Molan was an intriguing,<ref name="IA">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2002/09/imageattack20020926.html|series=Image Attack|text=Eye-Catching Style}}</ref> statuesque<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|75|Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night}}</ref> woman who was acquainted with Nyrat Agira and Di Mantid. While the three appeared to be friends, they were in fierce competition with each other for the unofficial title of "best dressed Outlander patron," a title Agira often won.<ref name="FF" /> She had [[Color/Legends|light]] skin, black [[Hair/Legends|hair]],<ref name="DB" /> and brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. At the Outlander Club, her hair was worn up in two conelike shapes<ref name="aotcvis" /> that were reminiscent of the [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|75|Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night}}</ref>

==Equipment==
While in the Outlander Club, Molan wore a sleek and shimmering<ref name="FF" /> blue dress,<ref name="aotcvis" /> a medallion<ref name="Insider" /> on her forehead, and silver height-enhancing shoes for hired dancers. She also wore [[thoadeye-style makeup]] around her eyes.<ref name="aotcvis" /> The outfit did not match the charisma of what Agira was wearing at the time.<ref name="FF" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The statuesque Wilst Molan is one of the hardest characters to spot in the Outlander.|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="Insider" />}}
Wilst Molan first appeared in the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']],<ref name="aotc" /> which was released on [[May 16]], [[2002]].<ref name="Year">''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]''</ref> The Outlander Club scenes including Molan, who was portrayed by an uncredited extra,<ref name="aotc" /> were shot at [[Fox Studios, Sydney]] on [[July 17]], [[2000]].<ref name="Mythmaking">''[[Mythmaking: Behind the Scenes of Attack of the Clones]]''</ref> Prior to the film's release, the character was first pictured in the documentary "[[Making Episode II Webdocs|A Twinkle Beyond Pluto]],"<ref name="ATBP">{{SW|url=video/a-twinkle-beyond-pluto|series=Making Episode II Webdocs|text=Part 5: A Twinkle Beyond Pluto}}</ref> released on [[July 31]], [[2001]],<ref name="ATBP Date">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/bts/me2/archive.html|text=''Making Episode II Webdocs'' archive}}</ref> and identified in the [[StarWars.com]] article "Making Up is Hard to Do: Lesley Vanderwalt," which was published on [[February 15]], 2002.<ref name="Lesley Vanderwalt">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/feature/20020215/index.html|text=Making Up is Hard to Do: Lesley Vanderwalt}}</ref> 

In an image in the web article "[[Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases]]," published on [[StarWars.com]] on [[May 2]], [[2003]], Molan was identified as Nogo Molana, a name which has not appeared in any other source.<ref name="crc">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/bts/article/f20030502/index.html|text=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases|int=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases}}</ref> In the [[April 6]], [[2004]]<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20040315.html|text=The New Face of Evil}}</ref> article "[[Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night]]," published in the [[Star Wars Insider 75|seventy-fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, author [[Daniel Wallace]] described Molan as one of the hardest characters to spot in the Outlander Club.<ref name="Insider" />
